column1: # MIND THE GAP: Argumentative Enthymeme Detection & Reconstruction

column2: end-to-end Machine Learning and NLP pipeline designed to detect missing argumentative premises (enthymemes) in student essays and logically reconstruct those missing gaps using Generative AI.

column1: The project is executed on a SLURM high-performance computing cluster and is divided into two major phases:

column3: and PageRank algorithms to identify the most logical hidden gaps in human arguments.

column1: 2. **Generative Reconstruction:** Fine-tuning a `facebook/bart-large` Auto-Encoder to read corrupted arguments and generate the missing logical bridges.
* **`data/`**: Contains `raw/` dataset inputs (v2-arguments) and `processed/` CSVs generated throughout the pipeline. *(Note: Data files are git-ignored).*
* **`models/`**: Stores the compiled Hugging Face model weights
column3: and output prediction CSVs. *(Note: Weights are git-ignored).*

column1: * **`sbatch_scripts/`**: Shell scripts configured to launch training and evaluation jobs on the `gammaweb` SLURM cluster.
* **`logs/`**: Standard output and error logs from SLURM jobs.

column2: we utilize `facebook/bart-large`. To ensure mathematical stability and prevent gradient explosion (NaN loss) during fine-tuning:

column1: * **Native Tokenization:** We use the standard string `[MISSING]` rather than adding special tokens
* **Full-Text Auto-Encoding:** The `target_text` is formatted as the *entire* unmasked paragraph
column2: perfectly aligning with BART's pre-trained auto-encoder instincts.

column1: * **Strict Optimizer Limits:** The learning rate is strictly capped at `4e-6` with an increased `adam_epsilon` of `1e-6`.

column2: predictions were filtered to extract *only* the generated gap text and compared against the original human sentences.

column1: The model successfully learned argumentative reasoning
column2: demonstrating distinct performance based on the contextual position of the missing premise/claim:

column1: | Gap Position | N | ROUGE-1 | ROUGE-2 | ROUGE-L |
*Note: In the field of open-ended argumentative generation
column2: a ~15% ROUGE-1 score represents a highly successful outcome. Qualitative analysis shows the model frequently invents valid
column3: novel arguments that logically fit the topic rather than simply memorizing the human author's exact phrasing.*
